ucs.title: [LLM] Salt Typhoon (Earth Estries / GhostEmperor) IIS w3wp.exe spawning shells or dropping ASPX after Exchange exploitation
ucs.description: Hunts the post-exploitation footprint Salt Typhoon repeatedly leaves on Microsoft Exchange and IIS at telco/government targets — w3wp.exe spawning command-shell / discovery LOLBins, or w3wp.exe writing .aspx/.ashx into OWA/ECP/Autodiscover paths. This is the chain Earth Estries / GhostEmperor used to seed Demodex, SparrowDoor and Crowdoor after exploiting ProxyLogon, ProxyShell and CVE-2023-23397 / CVE-2021-26855 on edge Exchange servers.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_name) as child from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" (Processes.parent_process="*MSExchange*" OR Processes.parent_process="*OWA*" OR Processes.parent_process="*ECP*" OR Processes.parent_process="*Autodiscover*" OR Processes.parent_process="*DefaultAppPool*") (Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | append [| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as paths from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.process_name="w3wp.exe" (Filesystem.file_path="*\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\aspnet_client\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Exchange Server\\V15\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Exchange Server\\V15\\ClientAccess\\*") (Filesystem.file_name="*.aspx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.ashx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.asax" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.asp") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`] | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let Shells = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","arp.exe","ping.exe"]);
let ShellSpawn = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("MSExchange","OWA","ECP","Autodiscover","DefaultAppPool")
    | where FileName in~ (Shells)
    | extend Signal = "w3wp_spawn_shell"
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, Signal,
              ChildImage = FileName, ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName, ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine, SHA256;
let WebshellDrop = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\", @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\", @"\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\", @"\Exchange Server\V15\Logging\")
    | where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx" or FileName endswith ".asax" or FileName endswith ".asp"
    | extend Signal = "w3wp_writes_webshell"
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName, Signal,
              ChildImage = FileName, ChildCmd = FolderPath,
              ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName, ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine, SHA256;
union ShellSpawn, WebshellDrop
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let Shells = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "w3wp.exe"
| extend NewProcessLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,"\\")[-1]))
| where NewProcessLeaf in~ (Shells)
| where Account !endswith "$"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ParentImage = ParentProcessName,
          ChildImage = NewProcessName,
          CommandLine,
          TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Salt Typhoon - IIS w3wp.exe Spawning Shell or LOLBin (Exchange Post-Exploitation)
id: 7f1c4b2a-9d3e-4e7c-9f51-saltyphoon0001
status: experimental
description: Detects Earth Estries / GhostEmperor / Salt Typhoon post-exploitation of Exchange and IIS where w3wp.exe spawns a shell or discovery LOLBin, the precursor to Demodex / SparrowDoor / Crowdoor deployment.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1045/
  - https://www.trendmicro.com/en_us/research/24/k/earth-estries.html
author: detection-engineering
date: 2026-05-06
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.g1045
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
  child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: parent and child and not filter_machine_acct
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate Exchange health probes shelling out (rare; tune by command line)
  - Admin-driven IIS troubleshooting
level: high
ucs.rationale: Salt Typhoon / Earth Estries / GhostEmperor / FamousSparrow are repeatedly attributed to ProxyLogon, ProxyShell and Outlook/Exchange chain exploitation against telcos and government — public reporting from Trend Micro, Microsoft, CISA and Sygnia ties their initial-access pattern specifically to w3wp.exe-spawned shells dropping ASPX webshells (SparrowDoor, China Chopper variants) into OWA/ECP/HttpP
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Salt Typhoon Demodex / SparrowDoor / Crowdoor DLL side-loading via signed binary in user-writable path
ucs.description: Hunts the actor's signature install pattern: a legitimately signed third-party binary (Trend Micro, Bitdefender, ESET, Notepad++ GUP.exe, Norton, Kaspersky) staged in C:\ProgramData, C:\Users\Public or AppData side-loads a co-located non-Microsoft DLL. This is how Earth Estries deploys Demodex, how FamousSparrow drops SparrowDoor/Crowdoor, and how GhostEmperor stages its rootkit loader.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as loader_path values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\PerfLogs\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Windows\\Temp\\*") (Processes.process_name IN ("GUP.exe","PwmSvc.exe","K7sysmon.exe","Mc.exe","GoogleUpdate.exe","vmnat.exe","BdeUISrv.exe","ESET.exe","AvastSvc.exe","Norton.exe","K3WLog.exe","K3WLOG.exe")) by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner host process_path [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Filesystem.file_name) as sideloaded_dll values(Filesystem.file_path) as dll_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action="created" Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" (Filesystem.file_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\PerfLogs\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Windows\\Temp\\*") by host Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name | rename Filesystem.* as * | rex field=file_path "(?<process_path>.*)\\\\[^\\\\]+$"] | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let AbusedSignedLoaders = dynamic([
    "GUP.exe",                  // Notepad++ updater (FamousSparrow / SparrowDoor)
    "K3WLog.exe", "K3WLOG.exe", // K7 Computing (Earth Estries / Demodex)
    "K7sysmon.exe",             // K7 AV
    "PwmSvc.exe",               // Bitdefender (Earth Estries)
    "BdeUISrv.exe",             // Bitdefender
    "vmnat.exe",                // VMware (multiple Chinese clusters)
    "GoogleUpdate.exe",         // Google updater
    "Mc.exe",                   // McAfee
    "OneDrive.exe",             // Microsoft (mimicked path)
    "Mobsync.exe",              // GhostEmperor side-loading
    "sllauncher.exe"            // Silverlight (FamousSparrow)
  ]);
let UserWritablePrefixes = dynamic([
    @"C:\ProgramData\", @"C:\Users\Public\", @"C:\PerfLogs\",
    @"C:\Windows\Temp\", @"C:\Intel\", @"C:\Recovery\"
  ]);
let Loaders = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where FileName in~ (AbusedSignedLoaders)
    | where FolderPath startswith @"C:\ProgramData\" or FolderPath startswith @"C:\Users\Public\"
         or FolderPath has @"\AppData\Local\Temp\" or FolderPath has @"\AppData\Roaming\"
         or FolderPath startswith @"C:\PerfLogs\" or FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\Temp\"
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              LoaderImage = FileName, LoaderFolder = FolderPath,
              LoaderSHA256 = SHA256, LoaderSigner = ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName,
              LoaderCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName, ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
let Sideload = DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where FileName endswith ".dll"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (AbusedSignedLoaders)
    | where FolderPath =~ InitiatingProcessFolderPath        // co-located DLL = side-load
    | where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\" or FolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files\" or FolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)\")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              LoaderImage = InitiatingProcessFileName, LoaderFolder = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
              SideloadedDll = FileName, SideloadedDllSHA256 = SHA256,
              LoaderSHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256;
Loaders
| join kind=inner Sideload on DeviceId, LoaderImage, LoaderFolder
| where Timestamp1 between (Timestamp .. Timestamp + 5m)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          LoaderImage, LoaderSigner, LoaderFolder, LoaderSHA256,
          SideloadedDll, SideloadedDllSHA256,
          ParentImage, ParentCmd
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let AbusedSignedLoaders = dynamic([
    "gup.exe","k3wlog.exe","k7sysmon.exe","pwmsvc.exe","bdeuisrv.exe",
    "vmnat.exe","googleupdate.exe","mc.exe","mobsync.exe","sllauncher.exe"
  ]);
ImProcessCreate
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| extend ImageLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(TargetProcessName,"\\")[-1]))
| where ImageLeaf in (AbusedSignedLoaders)
| where TargetProcessName has_any ("\\ProgramData\\","\\Users\\Public\\","\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\","\\AppData\\Roaming\\","\\PerfLogs\\","\\Windows\\Temp\\")
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| join kind=inner (
    ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified")
    | where TargetFileName endswith ".dll"
    | where TargetFilePath has_any ("\\ProgramData\\","\\Users\\Public\\","\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\","\\AppData\\Roaming\\","\\PerfLogs\\","\\Windows\\Temp\\")
    | project DllDropTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, TargetFilePath, TargetFileName, TargetFileSHA256
  ) on DvcHostname
| where DllDropTime between (TimeGenerated - 30m .. TimeGenerated + 5m)
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          LoaderImage = TargetProcessName, LoaderCmd = TargetProcessCommandLine,
          ParentImage = ParentProcessName,
          SideloadedDll = TargetFileName, DllPath = TargetFilePath, DllSHA256 = TargetFileSHA256
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Salt Typhoon - Signed Loader Side-Loading from User-Writable Path (Demodex / SparrowDoor / Crowdoor)
id: 7f1c4b2a-9d3e-4e7c-9f51-saltyphoon0002
status: experimental
description: Detects Salt Typhoon (Earth Estries / GhostEmperor / FamousSparrow) DLL side-loading where signed third-party binaries (Notepad++ GUP, K7, Bitdefender, McAfee, etc.) are staged outside Program Files and execute from ProgramData, Public, AppData, PerfLogs or Windows\Temp.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1045/
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/2021/09/23/famoussparrow-suspicious-hotel-guest/
  - https://www.trendmicro.com/en_us/research/24/k/earth-estries.html
author: detection-engineering
date: 2026-05-06
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.g1045
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  loader_name:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\GUP.exe'
      - '\K3WLog.exe'
      - '\K3WLOG.exe'
      - '\K7sysmon.exe'
      - '\PwmSvc.exe'
      - '\BdeUISrv.exe'
      - '\vmnat.exe'
      - '\GoogleUpdate.exe'
      - '\Mc.exe'
      - '\Mobsync.exe'
      - '\sllauncher.exe'
  loader_path:
    Image|contains:
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\AppData\Local\Temp\'
      - '\AppData\Roaming\'
      - '\PerfLogs\'
      - '\Windows\Temp\'
      - '\Intel\'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: loader_name and loader_path and not filter_machine_acct
falsepositives:
  - Portable application installs in non-standard locations
  - Endpoint security tool staging during a re-install
level: high
ucs.rationale: DLL side-loading is the single most consistent piece of Salt Typhoon tradecraft documented across vendor reporting: ESET on FamousSparrow's K3WLOG / sllauncher side-loading of SparrowDoor; Trend Micro on Earth Estries staging signed binaries plus DLLs in ProgramData / Users\Public to load Demodex, Crowdoor and SnappyBee; Kaspersky on GhostEmperor's signed-loader chain. Generic 'DLL side-loading' d
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
